title: Flock Audit - National Lookups

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Flock Audit - National Lookups
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

On March 9th, your agency issued a press release about the use of Flock Safety cameras by federal agencies. Please provide the following information relating to your Flock Safety system: 

1) Please provide any internal audits, reports, or memos written about the audit your agency conducted after finding out the about the "National Lookup" configuration issue in Flock Safety. 

2) Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

3) Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

4) A list of network share settings that include:
 - 4a. Flock networks shared with me
 - 4b. Networks that I’m sharing

Please provide records in their original electronic, machine readable format. For example, please provide csv or spreadsheet files where applicable. Please do not redirect me to your transparency portal. I appreciate you taking the time to complete this request.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Alan J. Segal

title: Metro Transit / Bi State Development Agency – Public Safety Records

communications.subject: The Missouri Sunshine Law Request: Metro Transit / Bi State Development Agency – Public Safety Records
communications.communication:
Attention: Custodian of Records/ Chief Legal Counsel 

Pursuant to the The Missouri Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

Legal Authority and Purpose:
This request is made pursuant to the Missouri Sunshine Law, Chapter 610, RSMo, which mandates public access to “records of public governmental bodies” unless specifically exempt (§ 610.011, RSMo).

The Bi State Development Agency (BSD), as operator of Metro Transit, performs core public transportation functions in Missouri using significant public funds. Accordingly, records concerning public safety, incident reports, and operational alerts on Metro Transit routes within Missouri are subject to disclosure under Chapter 610.

This request seeks to improve public transparency regarding safety on Metro Transit routes, specifically for passengers and community members using public transportation in the St. Louis region.

Requested Documents/Records:
Please provide copies of all records, electronic or paper, related to public safety, security incidents, alerts, or communications for the following specific Metro Transit bus routes in St. Louis, Missouri:
•	11 Chippewa Route
•	41 Lee Route 
•	74 Florissant Route 


Date Range: November 1, 2024 – the final date of completion for all processing of this request. 


A. Incident and Public Safety Logs
•	All incident reports, logs, or records generated by Metro Transit staff, security personnel, law enforcement, or other partners for these routes. 
•	Include events such as suspicious behavior, threats, assaults, disturbances, trespassing, medical emergencies, police responses, or other safety-related events. 

B. Rider Safety Alerts and Notifications
•	Public and internal notifications sent to riders or staff related to safety incidents on these routes. 
•	Drafts, emails, or records showing the creation and dissemination of alerts. 

C. Internal Communications About Safety and Security
•	Emails, memoranda, or reports between Metro Transit and law enforcement or other partners regarding these routes involving any previously listed events (suspicious behavior, threats, assaults, etc.). 
•	Any discussion of patterns, trends, or security planning affecting the 11 Chippewa, 41 Lee and 74 Florissant routes. 

D. Security Assessments, Audits, or Evaluations
•	Security reviews, audits, or assessments specific to the 11 Chippewa, 41 Lee and 74 Florissant routes, including risk mitigation or safety recommendations. 


Requested Format:
I am respectfully seeking all documents/records through MuckRock.com.

I am requesting that documents/records be sent:
•	Electronically, preferably as a searchable PDF file attachment or native electronic files (Excel, CSV). 
•	Records organized by route and date range, with an index identifying content. 
•	Redact only legally exempt personal information; provide the remainder of records. 

Per Section 610.024, RSMo, “If a public record contains material which is not exempt from disclosure as well as material which is exempt from disclosure, the public governmental body shall separate the exempt and nonexempt material and make the nonexempt material available for examination and copying.”

If portions of the requested documents/records are closed due to exemption, please separate, omit or redact only the closed portions and provide me with all segregable portions of the documents/records. I also request that you please provide a detailed justification for each redaction or omission, and cite the specific exemption used for each redacted or omitted portion of the original document/records.

Fee Waiver/Public Interest/Public Release:
As the records sought detail the operations and activities of an entity providing services on behalf of local government, I also request that fees be waived as this request is in the public’s interest. I am seeking the records to analyze, inform and freely release to members of the public as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com.

As stated in Section 610.022, RSMo, “public records shall be presumed to be open…documents may be furnished without charge or at a reduced charge when the public governmental body determines that waiver or reduction of the fee is in the public interest because it is likely to contribute significantly to the public understanding of the operations or activities of the public governmental body and is not primarily in the commercial interest of the requester.”  The subject of the requested documents/records will provide considerably increased insight into passenger and personnel safety during Metro Transit operations. The requested records are not for commercial use.

If fees cannot be waived, I am willing to pay all reasonable duplication expenses incurred in the processing this Missouri Sunshine Law request. However, as I am a member of the public and requesting documents specifically for the purpose of increasing the public’s knowledge of governmental functioning, I will administratively appeal any denial of my request for a fee waiver. If fees cannot be waived, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of completing fulfilment of my request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Kevin Hawkins

The City intends to cooperate to the fullest extent possible with the Public Records Act ("PRA") (Government Code section 7920.000 et seq.) City of Watsonville, CA ************************************************************************ Your record request #26-158 has been submitted successfully. Dear Requester:  The City of Watsonville ("City") is in receipt of the Public Records Act request you submitted. The City intends to cooperate to the fullest extent possible with the Public Records Act ("PRA") (Government Code section 7920.000 et seq.) Under established California law, the City is obliged to comply with a request for a public record so long as the requestor makes a specific and focused request for information, that information is maintained by the City in its ordinary course of business, the information is disclosable, and the record can be located with reasonable effort. The PRA provides for the inspection or copying of existing identifiable public records; it does not compel the City to respond directly to questions or create new records, lists, or reports in response to a request. Within the next ten (10) days, we will determine whether your request seeks copies of disclosable records in the City's possession or whether the City will require an extension. title: CIA support for 2024- series "The Agency"

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: CIA support for 2024- series "The Agency"
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All documents pertaining to collaboration between the Central Intelligence Agency (CIA) and the production of the TV series "The Agency" (2024-), starring Michael Fassbender. Such documents may include, but should not be not limited to, script notes; production assistance agreements; production stills; and all records of communications between employees of the CIA and members of the cast, crew, and production company of "The Agency," including memos, notes of meetings and conference calls, emails, and other forms of communication

I would also like to know if the CIA provided any form of support or technical advice whatsoever to the production of "The Agency" or to any member of the cast or crew, or if the production or any member of the cast or crew sought assistance from the CIA in connection with the production of the film.

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation. These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. Attached is a document with organization letterhead confirming my position, as well as the organization's 501(c)(3) application and approval form.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo

communications.communication:
Good morning Mr. Carollo 

This is to acknowledge the receipt of your request and to let you know that I am the analyst assigned for processing.  If you have any questions, please feel free to contact me via email or 703-614-4008. 

Please be aware that, whenever a FOIA requester has not received a determination within 20 working days from the date a request is perfected, they are entitled to challenge the delay.  To do this, you may submit an appeal within the SecureRelease online portal (www.securerelease.us) or by writing to the Department of the Navy, Office of the Judge Advocate General (Code 14), 1322 Patterson Ave SE, Suite 3000, Washington Navy Yard, DC 20374-5066.  If you prefer, you can also submit an appeal via email to Navy-OJAG-C14-FOIA-PA-ADMINAPPEALS@us.navy.mil.  Your appeal, if any, should include a statement indicating why you believe it should be granted.  It must be postmarked or timestamped within 90 calendar days from the date of this letter. 

You also have the right to seek assistance and/or dispute resolution services from the Department of the Navy FOIA Public Liaison, Mr. Christopher Julka, at Christopher.a.julka.civ@us.navy.mil or (703) 697-0031, and may also contact the Office of Government Information Services (OGIS) for assistance and/or dispute resolution at ogis@nara.gov or (877) 684-6448. 

Thank you for your patience as we work to complete the processing of your request.  Hope you have a great day. 

Sincerely,

title: Phone and text message records between police and ICE in Massachusetts (Boston Police Department)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:  

1. All departmental or municipal policies regarding cell phone use and procedures for retaining cell phone and landline phone data. The records request includes, but is not limited to, policies regarding: 

*   Officer or employee use of cellphones 
*       Officer use of social media 
*       Records retention practices of phone logs and text messages 
*       Mobile device (i.e. cell phone) management 
*       Authorized software & internet usage on department-issued devices 
*       Bringing your own device 

This request covers any policies that are currently in effect or that have been in effect at any point on or after January 20, 2025. 

2. Current contract and most recent procurement records related to managing mobile phone devices within your department. This includes information about the mobile device management system (e.g. Microsoft Intune, Panasonic Connect, etc.)  that is used by the department to manage employee cell phone and computer communications, as well as screenshots of how department mobile technology is centrally managed. 

3. Call logs, if any, from January 20, 2025, to the day this request is responded to, between a department phone (including both department provided cell phones and landlines) and the following numbers: 

*   (802) 872-6020   
*   (781) 359-7500  
*   (860) 244-0522 
*   (413) 221-1303 
*   (857) 416-3742 
*   (781) 359-7451 
*   (703) 725-5982 
*   (781) 359-7553 
*   (781) 917-5143 
*   (857) 416-3705 
*   (603) 493-4520 

  Any other phone number known by the department to be a phone contact for Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) or Customs and Border Patrol (CBP). 

4. Text message records from department issued cell phones (including SMS, RCS, and iMessage) communications sent or received on department issued mobile devices from January 20, 2025, to the date this request is responded to, that contain any of the following terms: 

*       ICE 
*       CBP 
*       Immigration 
*       Immigrant 
*       Detainer 

This request includes messages stored on devices themselves, in any backup system, cloud storage, mobile device management platform, or held by any third-party vendor on behalf of the department. 

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as this request is in the public interest. I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents. In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available.  

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.  I am happy to discuss this request with your staff to help clarify any matters. 

 

Sincerely, 

Joshua Dankoff 
Director of Strategic Initaitives 
Citizens for Juvenile Justice 
joshuadankoff@cfjj.org  
(submitted via muckrock)
